mapper.update: update batch.worker_registry set status = 'DECOMMISSIONED', heartbeat_at = current_timestamp, drain_started_at = null, drain_deadline_at = null, updated_at = current_timestamp where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and worker_code = #{workerCode}

update batch.worker_registry set status = 'OFFLINE', updated_at = current_timestamp where status in ('ONLINE', 'DRAINING') and heartbeat_at < current_timestamp - (#{timeoutSeconds} * interval '1 second')
mapper.sql: id, tenant_id, worker_code, worker_group, capability_tags::text as capability_tags_text, resource_tag, status, heartbeat_at, current_load, max_concurrent, drain_started_at, drain_deadline_at, host_name, host_ip, process_id, build_id, sdk_version
mapper.select: select count(1) from batch.worker_registry where tenant_id = #{tenantId} and status = #{status}
select count(1) from batch.worker_registry where status = 'DRAINING' and drain_deadline_at is not null and drain_deadline_at < current_timestamp
select count(1) from batch.worker_registry where status = 'ONLINE' and ( heartbeat_at is null or heartbeat_at < current_timestamp - (#{timeoutSeconds} * interval '1 second') )

select count(distinct wr.id) from batch.worker_registry wr join batch.job_task jt on jt.tenant_id = wr.tenant_id and jt.assigned_worker_code = wr.worker_code where wr.status = 'DECOMMISSIONED' and jt.task_status in ('CREATED', 'READY', 'RUNNING')

select count(1) from batch.worker_registry where status in ('ONLINE', 'DRAINING') and capability_tags is not null and ( case when jsonb_typeof(capability_tags) = 'array' then exists ( select 1 from jsonb_array_elements(capability_tags) elem where jsonb_typeof(elem) <> 'string' ) else true end )
mapper.insert:
insert into batch.worker_registry ( tenant_id, worker_code, worker_group, capability_tags, resource_tag, status, heartbeat_at, current_load, max_concurrent, drain_started_at, drain_deadline_at, host_name, host_ip, process_id, build_id, sdk_version, created_at, updated_at ) values ( #{tenantId}, #{workerCode}, #{workerGroup}, cast(#{capabilityTags.value} as jsonb), #{resourceTag},
#{status} 'ONLINE' ,
#{heartbeatAt} current_timestamp ,
#{currentLoad} 0 ,
#{maxConcurrent} 10 , #{drainStartedAt}, #{drainDeadlineAt}, #{hostName}, #{hostIp}, #{processId}, #{buildId}, #{sdkVersion}, current_timestamp, current_timestamp ) on conflict (tenant_id, worker_code) do nothing
mapper.update: update batch.worker_registry set worker_group = #{workerGroup}, capability_tags = cast(#{capabilityTags.value} as jsonb), resource_tag = #{resourceTag}, status = #{status}, heartbeat_at = #{heartbeatAt}, current_load = #{currentLoad}, max_concurrent = coalesce(#{maxConcurrent}, max_concurrent), drain_started_at = #{drainStartedAt}, drain_deadline_at = #{drainDeadlineAt}, host_name = coalesce(#{hostName}, host_name), host_ip = coalesce(#{hostIp}, host_ip), process_id = coalesce(#{processId}, process_id), build_id = coalesce(#{buildId}, build_id), sdk_version = coalesce(#{sdkVersion}, sdk_version), updated_at = current_timestamp where id = #{id}
mapper.select: select tenant_id, worker_code, capability_tags::text as raw_value from batch.worker_registry where status in ('ONLINE', 'DRAINING') and capability_tags is not null and ( jsonb_typeof(capability_tags) <> 'array' or exists ( select 1 from jsonb_array_elements(capability_tags) elem where jsonb_typeof(elem) <> 'string' ) )
